Synthetic Canadians: Comparing Synthetic Survey Data Generators

2020 Democracy Checkup — Political Attitudes Validation Study

Author

Synthetic Opinion Lab

Published

March 14, 2026

Overview

This report validates three synthetic data generation approaches against the 2020 Democracy Checkup (DC20), collected during the COVID-19 pandemic (n = 4,043).

The 19 variables span five substantive domains: government satisfaction, institutional confidence (5 institutions, including public health officers), political efficacy, immigration attitudes, social conservatism, and economic positions, plus party identification and vote choice.

Three generators are compared:

Generator N synthetic Overall quality score
LLM (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) 100 0.749
IRT (Graded Response Model) 1,000 0.569
Agent (Opinion dynamics) 500 0.531

Correlation Structure

Frobenius distance from real correlation matrix:
  IRT                 : Frobenius = 4.09, r(lower tri) = 0.283
  LLM                 : Frobenius = 5.00, r(lower tri) = 0.595
  Agent               : Frobenius = 13.70, r(lower tri) = 0.086

Regression Replication: Vote Choice Predictors

Summary

Dimension Real IRT LLM Agent
N respondents 4,043 1,000 100 500
Overall quality score — 0.569 0.749 0.531
Valid response range ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅
Mean-level accuracy — Partial Good Partial
Variance accuracy — Good Moderate Moderate
Correlation structure — Moderate Best Weak

LLM achieves the highest fidelity (0.749). IRT (0.569) produces good variance. Agent (0.531) converges to moderate uniform intensity. Note: DC20 adds confidence in public health officers (inst_5), reflecting the COVID-19 context.
